description: Execute central bank monetary policy using open market operations, policy rate, reserve requirements, and standing facilities to meet inflation and financial stability objectives

fields.name: action_amount
fields.label: Change in rate or balance sheet amount

objectives.price_stability: Core mandate — keep inflation near target (commonly 2 percent)
objectives.financial_stability: Safeguard the banking system and smooth market functioning

primary_tools.policy_rate: Target short-term interbank rate (e.g., federal funds rate)
primary_tools.open_market_operations: Buy/sell securities to adjust bank reserves
primary_tools.reserve_requirement: Fraction of deposits banks must hold at the central bank
primary_tools.unconventional: Large-scale asset purchases (QE), yield-curve control, forward guidance

transmission_mechanism.interest_rate_channel: Policy rate -> bank lending rates -> consumption and investment
transmission_mechanism.asset_price_channel: Rate changes -> bond, equity, and housing valuations
transmission_mechanism.exchange_rate_channel: Rate changes -> capital flows -> FX -> net exports
transmission_mechanism.credit_channel: Balance-sheet strength and bank lending capacity
transmission_mechanism.expectations_channel: Forward guidance anchors future rate path and inflation expectations

inflation_targeting.independence: Insulates policy from short-run political pressure
inflation_targeting.accountability: Clear metric to judge central bank performance

limitations.zero_lower_bound: Rates cannot be cut much below zero
limitations.supply_shocks: Monetary policy cannot offset supply-side inflation without recession risk
limitations.banking_transmission: Weak bank balance sheets impair transmission

validation.valid_tool: tool in {policy_rate, open_market_ops, reserve_requirement, standing_facility, asset_purchases}

errors.message: tool must be policy_rate, open_market_ops, reserve_requirement, standing_facility, or asset_purchases

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume monetary policy can offset large supply shocks without growth costs
anti_patterns.why: Tightening into supply-side inflation reduces employment and output; central banks face a genuine trade-off

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore the zero lower bound
anti_patterns.why: Below ZLB, conventional rate cuts lose force; policy must pivot to QE, forward guidance, or fiscal coordination

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat all central banks as equally credible
anti_patterns.why: Independence and track record vary; markets price differently for high-credibility vs low-credibility regimes

anti_patterns.rule: Do not conflate price-level control with financial stability
anti_patterns.why: Low inflation can coexist with asset bubbles; macroprudential tools often needed alongside rates

goals.description: Execute central bank monetary policy using open market operations, policy rate, reserve requirements, and standing facilities to meet inflation and financial stability objectives

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

contractionary_vs_expansionary.contractionary: Raise rate, sell securities, increase RR, tighten standing facilities
contractionary_vs_expansionary.expansionary: Cut rate, buy securities, lower RR, loosen standing facilities

policy_interaction_with_fiscal.consistent: Both expansionary or both contractionary -> large impact
policy_interaction_with_fiscal.opposed: Conflicting policies mute each other (e.g., loose fiscal + tight monetary)
